name: Wnb Financial, N.A.
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving Minnesota and Wisconsin with multiple branch locations, personal and business banking, trust services, and financial planning.
description_long: WNB Financial, N.A. is a community bank operating in the Upper Midwest, primarily serving the Winona, Minnesota area and surrounding regions in Minnesota and Wisconsin. The bank maintains FDIC insurance, providing deposit protection backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. WNB Financial operates as a traditional full-service bank with a physical branch network rather than a digital-only institution. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking services including personal checking accounts, savings products, business banking with digital access, trust and estate management, retirement planning, and financial planning services. Customers can access accounts through multiple channels: WNB Digital (online/mobile banking), in-person at branch locations, and by phone. The bank provides 401(k) account access and operates an ADVICEWORKS® advisory service for investment and financial guidance. WNB Financial distinguishes itself through its community presence with seven physical branch locations across Minnesota and Wisconsin (Downtown Winona, East Winona, West Winona, Holmen, Kellogg, Wabasha, and Altoona offices), extended hours including Saturday service at most locations, and a dedicated ATM network. The bank emphasizes fraud prevention education and maintains a career-focused culture highlighted on their website. Their routing number is 091900216, and they operate a toll-free line (1-800-546-4392) for customer service. As a regional community bank, WNB Financial serves customers seeking traditional banking with local branch access and personalized service. However, customers requiring extensive national branch networks or cutting-edge fintech features may find more robust options elsewhere. The bank's geographic concentration in the Upper Midwest limits accessibility for customers outside this region.
services: Personal checking accounts with multiple tiers
Digital banking via WNB Digital platform
Business checking and business banking services
Financial planning via ADVICEWORKS® advisory service
Fraud prevention education and scam identification resources
ATM network across branch locations and partner grocery stores
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. Government backing
Seven physical branch locations with extended hours including Saturday service
Multiple account access channels: online (WNB Digital), mobile, phone, and in-person
Trust and estate planning services available alongside retirement planning
Fraud prevention education resources to help customers identify and avoid scams
Dedicated business banking platform with digital access for business customers
Local community bank with established presence in Winona since at least the branch office era
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Minnesota and Wisconsin—not accessible to customers outside the Upper Midwest
No mention of competitive rates, APY tiers, or rate comparisons on website—actual rates require viewing separate rates page
Minimal information about minimum account balances, monthly fees, or account maintenance costs
No details provided about loan products (mortgages, auto loans, personal loans) or lending rates
Limited digital-first features compared to neobanks; appears to be traditional banking model without modern fintech emphasis
best_for: Customers in Minnesota and Wisconsin seeking FDIC-insured banking with physical branch access
Small business owners needing local business banking relationships and in-person service
Individuals interested in trust, estate planning, and retirement planning services with financial advisor guidance
Customers who prefer traditional banking with Saturday hours and multiple convenient branch locations
diagnosis: WNB Financial is best for Upper Midwest residents and businesses seeking traditional FDIC-insured community banking with in-person branch relationships, trust services, and financial planning support. The primary caveat is limited geographic reach—this bank only serves Minnesota and Wisconsin, making it inaccessible to customers outside this region who cannot visit physical branches.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: FDIC-insured community bank serving Minnesota and Wisconsin with personal and business banking, trust services, and financial planning.
